# Transcrição: 01- Rapid Fire Testing - How it Works

- **Duração:** 04:50

## [00:00 - 03:00]

All right, welcome to the launching section where I'm going to be taking you through a process that we use essentially for pretty much saving a lot of time and money when we're working with client campaigns. So this training is going to be applicable to you if you are taking on a brand new ad account so nothing has been done before. There's no previous data and you're starting from scratch. It's also going to work if you're taking over an account that isn't doing so well. So things need to be rebuilt from that point forward. And this will also work really well if your client's coming to you saying, hey, we want to launch something or we want to test a new product or a new offer or an audience. And hey, even if you're just trying to actually optimize what the client is already doing, this is going to work. Now the process that we use is something called rapid fire testing. I don't want you to get caught up on the name. Lots of people have different terminology for this very same approach, but it is something that the top media buyers in the industry will use and we so happen to call it rapid fire testing. So it's essentially a process that we're going to use to validate audiences, funnels, and creative so that we save time and money so that you're not having to spend thousands of dollars collecting data before you can make a call if it's going to work or not so that you're not having to build out all of these funnels and email sequences and lead magnets, which takes so much time and energy until you actually validate that it's actually going to work. So I'm going to show you guys an overview of how this process works and then what you're going to find is a step by step on how to set up each round. So this is made up of three rounds. In the first round, what we're doing is we're essentially validating the personas within an audience. Now, please don't get mistaken personas is not targeting. So you're going to see when I explain this under the training for the first round of rapid fire testing exactly what those are and how to segment those out. Once we have identified these, we will then take this persona into the second round of rapid fire testing. So now that we have our persona, we now test out different lead magnets or offers or products to them to see which one is showing the most promise. Once we have this data and this Intel and we've completed the second round of rapid fire testing, we then take that lead magnet or that offer or that product into the third round where now we're going to test out different ad formats, different creatives, different

## [03:00 - 04:50]

headlines, different copy. We're going to test out, does it make a difference if we have emojis, no emojis? If your clients have multiple Facebook pages running it from certain pages, does that make an impact? So that's where you test out a lot of the creative in the last round. So you can consider the last round to be almost an optimization round because by the second round, you should have a fairly good idea of what's going to work. And once you're in the third round, you've got your funnel built out and really it's a matter of optimizing what you've already done at the beginning. Now this whole process, you can get away with spending between, I mean I would say just to be on the safe side, try to spend at least $200 to $300 on this rapid fire testing. Now if you're speaking to clients, I would highly recommend you get them to at least give you $500 for this testing round. The more money you have, the more data you're going to be putting through. So that's, I mean it's always a good thing, right? So don't just pinch pennies if you don't necessarily need to. However, if your client doesn't have a big budget or if yourself, if you don't have a big budget, know that you can still get really effective results from this even from a $200 total spend. I wouldn't really recommend going underneath that. I just, you're going to be doing a lot of assumptions there which we all know what they say about assumptions. Alright so that is the process that we're going to be using for launching campaigns, fixing existing stuff, taking new products to market or just optimizing what clients are doing. I'm going to take you guys into the first round of rapid fire testing.
